mnemonics: S.I.M.O.S. - Shared Images Mean Open Security (risks). Remember that sharing an image is like sharing an OS key, which opens doors to risks.
keyPoints: Shared VM images can contain pre-installed, vulnerable software or misconfigurations that get propagated across an organization.
The management OS (or hypervisor) has elevated access to all guest VMs; a compromise here can lead to a full infrastructure breach.
A maliciously modified public image from a marketplace can serve as a backdoor into any cloud environment that uses it.
commonMistakes: Assuming images from a trusted public marketplace are automatically secure and require no vetting.
Neglecting to scan or harden customized images before sharing them, treating them as 'golden' without ongoing maintenance.
Underestimating the attack surface of the management plane, focusing only on guest VM security.
